codeReview.summary: The feature centralizes lifecycle identity classification behind a single helper and tightens close-accounting to ignore non-user windows and only exit after the last visible user window closes. The Rust regression coverage directly exercises the claimed label-classification, reopen, and close-accounting invariants, and I did not find contract-breaking issues in the reviewed scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The window-lifecycle-worker skill still mandates a manual  un tauri dev verification step, but this mission explicitly defers desktop unsafe validation to a later manual pass. That requirement does not match current mission reality for code-reviewable lifecycle features.

summary: Pass. Reviewed the worker transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit b824247ba905f12e7114aaa26cd0180e634ce01. The implementation satisfies the scoped lifecycle identity and close-accounting contracts in code: one authoritative label classification path now drives both WindowKindInfo and user-window checks, and exit decisions are based on post-close visible user-window counts. No blocking or non-blocking code issues found in the reviewed feature.
